323.010
DEFINITIONS .
As used in this chapter , unless the context requires
otherwise:
(1) " Board" means the State Board of Examiners
and Registration of Architects of Kentucky ;
(2) An " architect " is a natural person proficient in
the art of planning, designing , specifying , supervising
and administering the construction of buildings and
who is licensed to practice architecture ;
(3) The " practice of architecture " consists of per-
forming or offering to perform any professional serv-
ice involving consultations , planning , designing , spe-
cifying, responsible supervision or administration of
the construction of any building or additions or
alterations thereto ;
(4) A "building" is a structure erected as a shelter
for human beings , their activities and possessions .
(1960 , с . 218 , § 1; effective June 16, 1960 )
323.020 [ 73-1 ]
LICENSE REQUIRED .
Except as otherwise provided hereinafter , no per-
son shall practice architecture in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky without first obtaining a license under
the provisions of this chapter , it being the purpose
of this chapter to safeguard the life, health , property
and welfare of the public . (1960 , c. 218 , § 2 ; effec-
tive June 16, 1960 )
323.030 [ 73-6 ]
PERSONS AND BUILDINGS TO WHICH
CHAPTER DOES NOT APPLY
(1) A non - resident licensed to practice architecture
in his own state or country but not in the Common-
wealth and having no established place of business
5

in the Commonwealth may act solely as a consulting
associate of an architect licensed in the Common-
wealth .
(2) An architect acting solely as an officer or em-
ploye of the United States Government is not re-
quired to be licensed in the Commonwealth .
(3) A licensed professional engineer may prepare
plans and specifications for and supervise the con-
struction of structures as an incident to the practice
of his own profession .
(4) If the drawings and specifications are signed by
the authors thereof with the true titles of their occu-
pations as may be required by law , this chapter does
not apply to :
(a) Any building which is to be used for farm pur-
poses only;
(b) Any building containing not more than 20,000
square feet total floor area , except :
1. An asylum , hospital , nursing or convalescent home ,
or home for the aged , regardless of capacity ;
2. A school or educational institution , regardless of
capacity;
3. A residential building containing more than twelve
bedrooms ;
4. A place of assembly , regardless of capacity;
5. A mercantile building having a capacity in excess
of one hundred persons ;
6. An industrial building having a capacity in excess
of one hundred persons ; and
7. An office building having a capacity in excess of
one hundred persons ;
(c) Alterations to any building to which this chapter
does not apply , if the alterations do not involve
changes affecting structural safety . (1960 , c . 218 , § 3 ;
effective June 16, 1960 )
6

323.040 [ 73-7 ]
PREPARING PLANS AND SPECIFICA-
TIONS , WHEN PERMITTED BY OTHERS
THAN ARCHITECTS . REPEALED , 1960 , с .
218 , § 23 ; EFFECTIVE JUNE 16 , 1960 .
323.050 [ 73-5 ]
QUALIFICATIONS FOR LICENSE ; TIME
OF EXAMINATIONS .
( 1) To obtain a license to practice architecture in
Kentucky an applicant must satisfactorily pass an
examination as prescribed by the board , except as
otherwise provided in this chapter .
(2) Every applicant for examination shall fulfill the
following requirements :
(a) Must be of good moral character ; and
(b) Must be a citizen of the United States of America
and at least twenty -five years of age ; and either
(c) Must be a graduate of an accredited school of
architecture with at least three years' additional
architectural experience satisfactory to the board ; or
(d) Must be a graduate of an accredited high school
or have had education equivalent thereto as deter-
mined by the board , and , in addition , at least five
years ' varied architectural experience satisfactory to
the board .
(3) Examinations shall be given at least semi-
annually , the time and place to be established by
the board . ( 1960 , c . 218 , § 4; effective June 16,
1960 )
323.060 [ 73-5 ]
PERSONS WHO MAY BE LICENSED WITH-
OUT EXAMINATION .
Any person who is a licensed architect in another
state or country where the qualifications prescribed
7

at the time of licensing were , in the opinion of the
board, equal to those prescribed in the Common-
wealth at the date of application, and where recipro-
cal licensing privileges satisfactory to the board are
granted to licensees of the Commonwealth , may be
granted a license without an examination . ( 1960 , c .
218 , § 5 ; effective June 16 , 1960 )
323.070 [73-5 ]
PERSONS FOR WHOM EXAMINATION RE-
QUIRED . REPEALED , 1960, с. 218 , § 23 ;
EFFECTIVE JUNE 16, 1960.
323.080 [73-11 ; 73-13 ]
FEES .
( 1) Fees for the following services shall be established
by the rules of the board , and in no case shall they
exceed the following schedule :
(a) For an examination
(b) For a license certificate
(c) For the restoration of an expired license
(d) For a license to an architect satisfactorily
licensed in another state or country
$50.00
25.00
25.00
50.00
(2) The proper fee as prescribed above shall be
paid to the board , and shall not be refunded in
whole or in part . ( 1960 , c. 218 , § 6; effective June
16, 1960)
323.090 [ 73-11 ]
REEXAMINATION .
Any applicant who fails to pass an examination may,
without paying an additional fee, enter any sub-
sequent examination held within three years from
the date of the examination he failed to pass . (1960 ,
c . 218, § 7; effective June 16, 1960 )
8

323.095
SEAL REQUIRED ; USE .
(1) Each architect licensed for practice within the
Commonwealth shall secure an embossed circular
seal of the design prescribed by regulation of the
board .
(2) All working drawings, specifications and reports
prepared by, or under the supervision of the in-
dividual , partnership or firm , must bear the imprint
of this seal .
(3) No architect shall sign or affix his seal to any
drawings , specifications or reports which have not
been prepared under his immediate supervision .
(1960 , с . 218 , § 8 , effective June 16, 1960 )
323.100 [73-4 ]
FORM OF LICENSE CERTIFICATE .
A license certificate shall be signed by the president
and the secretary of the board and bear
itsseal
.
323.110
[73-13
]
RENEWAL CERTIFICATE
; VOLUNTARY
SURRENDER OF LICENSE
.
(1)
(
a) All architects desiring
tocontinue practice
shall annually
, prior
toJuly
1, secure from the board
arenewal certificate upon the payment
of
afee
of
not more than fifteen dollars
. Not later than June 15
ofeach year the board shall notify by mail all
architects
ofthe renewal date and fee
. Application
for
arenewal shall be upon such form
as
ispre-
scribed by the board and the architect shall furnish
the information required by such form
.
(b) Failure
ofany architect
tosecure his renewal
certificate within sixty days after July
1 shall con-
stitute sufficient cause for the board to revoke his
license
.
9

(c) The board may restore a license that has been
revoked for failure to pay the renewal fee, after one
year from the date of revocation and upon the pay-
ment of all delinquent fees .
(2) A licensed architect may voluntarily surrender
his license to the board and thereby be relieved of
the annual renewal fee . After surrendering his
li-
cense
,he shall not be known
as
alicensed architect
and shall desist from the practice thereof
. Within
five years from the time
ofsurrender
ofthe license
he may again qualify for
alicense without examina-
tion by the payment of the required fee
.
Iffive
years thereafter have elapsed
, he shall return
tothe
status
of
anew applicant
.
(1960
,
с. 218
,
§
9; effec-
tive June 16
,1960
)
323.120
[73-12
]
GROUNDS FOR REVOCATION OR SUSPEN-
SION
.
(1)The board may refuse
torenew
ormay suspend
orrevoke the license
ofany architect
topractice
architecture
inthe Commonwealth
ofKentucky for
any of the following reasons
:
(a)Gross incompetency
orgross negligence
inthe
planning
orconstruction
ofbuildings
,
asdetermined
by the board
;
(b)Unprofessional conduct
,
orconduct tending
to
bring the profession into disrepute
,
asdetermined
by the board
;
(c)Conviction
of
afelony
;
(d) Fraudulent
ordishonest architectural practice
;
(e)Use
offalse evidence
ormisrepresentations
inan
application for licensing
;
(f)Signing
oraffixing his seal
toany plans
, prints
,
specifications
ofbuildings
,
orreports
,which have not
been prepared by him personally
orby his employes
under his supervision
.
10

(2) The board shall revoke the license of an archi-
tect who practices architecture while his license is
suspended . ( 1960 , c. 218 , § 10; effective June 16 ,
1960)
323.130 [ 73-12 ]
HEARINGS , NOTICE IN REVOCATION PRO-
CEEDINGS .
( 1) No license shall be revoked or suspended without
a hearing . The board may proceed against a
licensee on its own initiative , on the basis of either
information contained in its own records or informa-
tion obtained through
itsinformal investigation
.
When
a formal complaint verified by affidavit
is
filed with the board by
a responsible citizen
or
organization
, containing allegations that
if true
would warrant suspension
orrevocation
of
alicense
,
the board shall proceed against the licensee within
three months
.
(2)
Awritten notice stating grounds for possible sus-
pension
orrevocation
ofthe license
,setting forth with
reasonable particularity the facts constituting the
alleged offense and the time and place
ofthe
hearing before the board on the charges
, shall be
served on the accused not less than twenty days
prior
tothe date
ofthe hearing either personally
or
by mailing
acopy thereof by registered mail
tothe
address of the accused last known to the board
.
(1960
,
с. 218
,
§
11
;effective June
16, 1960
)
323.140
[73-12
]
PRODUCTION OF EVIDENCE
; RIGHTS OF
ACCUSED
.
(1) The board
,
orany member thereof
, may issue
subpoenas
tocompel the attendance
ofwitnesses and
the production
ofdocuments
, and may administer
oaths
,take testimony
, hear proof and receive exhibits
inevidence
.
Incase
ofdisobedience
to
asubpoena
11

the board may invoke the aid of the Franklin Circuit
Court. Any order or subpoena of such court re-
quiring the attendance and testimony of witnesses
and the production of documentary evidence may be
enforced and shall be valid anywhere in this state .
(2) At any hearing the accused may appear in per-
son or by counsel , produce evidence and witnesses on
hisown behalf , cross -examine witnesses , and examine
such evidence and documents as may be produced
against him . The accused shall be entitled , on ap-
plication to the board , to the issuance of subpoenas
to compel the attendance of witnesses on his behalf .
The board may also be represented by counsel .
(3) The board shall not be bound by technical rules
of evidence , but the order of the board shall be
based on competent evidence .
(4) A stenographic record of the proceedings shall
be kept, but it shall not be necessary to transcribe
shorthand notes unless an appeal is taken from the
order .
(5) The board's order shall be in writing and shall
be accompanied by findings of fact and conclusions
oflaw.
(6) A copy of an order suspending or revoking a
license shall be served upon the licensee by registered
mail immediately upon entry by the board . Any
license that is suspended or revoked shall be sur-
rendered to the board accompanied by the licensee's
certificate . ( 1960 , c. 218, § 12; effective June 16,
1960)
323.145
APPEAL ; FRANKLIN CIRCUIT COURT ;
COURT OF APPEALS .
(1) Any person whose license is revoked by the board
may appeal from the order of revocation by filing
in the Franklin Circuit Court a petition for appeal
12

and a copy of the order of the board within sixty
days of the date of entry of the order . He shall
make bond for costs . The petition for appeal shall
set forth with particularity the grounds upon which
the appeal is taken . The licensee shall cause a copy
of the petition for appeal to be served upon the
secretary of the board .
(2) Within thirty days after service of notice of
appeal , or within such further time as the court may
allow , the board shall file in the Franklin Circuit
Court the original or a certified copy of the entire
record of the proceeding under review ; but, by
stipulation of the parties , the record may be
shortened .
(3) The filing of the petition for appeal shall not
stay enforcement of the order; but upon motion of
appellant the Franklin Circuit Court, in
itsdiscretion
,
may order
astay pending decision
ofthe appeal
.
If
the time for renewal of the license occurs during
a
stay
ofenforcement
,the board shall grant
arenewal
upon application properly made and payment
ofthe
usual fees
.
(4)The Franklin Circuit Court shall set the case for
hearing without
ajury and dispose
ofthe appeal
in
asummary manner
, being confined
tothe record
made before the board except that new evidence
may be introduced
as
tofraud
. The court may hear
oral arguments and receive written briefs
. The court
may affirm
orset aside the board's order
,
ormay
remand the case
tothe board for further evidence
,
but shall be limited
inmaking
itsdecision
todeter-
mining whether or not
:
(a)There
isany substantial evidence
tosupport the
order
;or
(b)The board acted without or
inexcess of
its
powers
;or
(c) The order appealed from was procured by fraud
.
(5)Any aggrieved party may secure
areview
of
an
adverse judgment
ofthe Franklin Circuit Court under
13

this chapter by appeal to the Court of Appeals .
Such appeal shall be taken in the manner provided
in the Rules of Civil Procedure . (1960 , c. 218 , § 13 ;
effective June 16, 1960)
323.150 [ 73-2 ]
MEMBERSHIP OF BOARD .
The board shall consist of five members , four of
whom shall be appointed by the Governor . The
fifth member of the board shall be the Dean of the
College of Engineering of the University of Kentucky .
But if at any time a five -year professional curriculum
of architecture is established in the Commonwealth
ofKentucky , the head of such curriculum shall replace
the Dean of the College of Engineering of the Uni-
versity of Kentucky as a member of the board . ( 1960 ,
c. 218, § 14 ; effective June 16, 1960 )
323.160 [ 73-2 ]
BOARD MEMBERS ; APPOINTMENT ;
TERMS .
The Governor shall appoint one member of the board
each year . Each member shall serve for a term of
four years , and until his successor is appointed and
qualified .
323.170 [ 73-2 ]
QUALIFICATIONS OF BOARD MEMBERS .
All members of the board appointed by the Governor
shall be architects registered in the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and shall have been in the active practice
of architecture for at least ten years immediately
preceding their appointment , and shall have been
residents of this Commonwealth for at least five
years immediately preceding their appointment .
(1960 , с. 218 , § 15; effective June 16, 1960 )
14

323.180 [ 73-3 ]
ELECTION OF OFFICERS .
The board , at the first meeting of each fiscal year ,
shall elect a president , secretary and treasurer . The
offices of secretary and treasurer may be held by the
same person . (1960 , c. 218 , § 16 ; effective June 16 ,
1960)
323.190 [ 73-12 ]
COMPENSATION OF BOARD MEMBERS .
Each member of the board shall receive an amount
not to exceed twenty -five dollars for each day or
part thereof spent in the performance of his official
duties , including time spent in necessary travel , and
in addition , shall be reimbursed for all proper travel-
ing and incidental expenses incurred in connection
with said duties . (1960 , c. 218 , § 17; effective June
16, 1960 )
323.200 [73-4]
QUORUM .
A quorum of the board shall consist of at least three
members . The concurring votes of three members
shall be considered as the action of the board , ex-
cept in the case of revoking a license , in which case ,
the unanimous vote of the board shall be required .
(1960, с. 218 , § 18 ; effective June 16 , 1960 )
323.210 [ 73-4]
FUNCTIONS OF BOARD .
(1) The board shall :
(a) Adopt and provide itself with a seal with a band
inscribed , " State Board of Examiners and Registra-
tion of Architects of Kentucky " with the coat of arms
ofthe state in the center ;
15

(b) Make all necessary regulations concerning the
contents and conduct of examinations , the method
and time for filing applications for examinations and
the time within which an applicant shall be examined
after his application has been filed;
(c) Keep a complete record of
itsproceedings and
an accurate list
ofall applications made
, licenses
issued and licenses revoked
; and
(d)Make
ageneral report including finances
tothe
Governor annually
.
(2) The board may adopt all reasonable regulations
not inconsistent with this chapter that are necessary
tocarry into effect the purposes
ofthis chapter
.
(1960
,
c. 218
,
§ 19
;effective June 16
,1960
)
323.220
[73-7
]
UNLICENSED PERSON MAY WORK UN-
DER ARCHITECT
. REPEALED
, 1960
,
с. 218
,
§ 23
; EFFECTIVE JUNE 16
, 1960
.
323.230
[73-8
]
WHO MAY BE STYLED AS ARCHITECTS
.
Only persons licensed under this chapter may be
styled
orknown
asarchitects
inKentucky
,
oruse
any words
orletters
orassume any titles
ordescrip-
tion tending
toconvey the impression that they are
architects
.
(1960
,
c. 218
,
§20
; effective June 16
,
1960
)
323.240
[73-7
]
CHAPTER NOT TO APPLY TO CERTAIN
BUILDING IN RURAL DISTRICTS
. RE-
PEALED
, 1960
,
c. 218
,
§
23; EFFECTIVE
JUNE 16
, 1960
.
16

323.250
INJUNCTION .
Whenever in the judgment of the board any person
has engaged , or is about to engage, in any acts or
practices that constitute , or will constitute , a violation
of KRS 323.020 , the board may inform the Attorney
General , who may make application to the Franklin
Circuit Court for an order enjoining such acts or prac-
tices , or the board may inform the Commonwealth's
attorney of the county in which the acts or practices
are occurring or will occur who may make applica-
tion to the circuit court of that county for an order
enjoining such acts or practices . Upon a showing
by the board that such person has engaged , or is
about to engage, in any such acts or practices , an
injunction , restraining order, or such other order as
may be appropriate shall be granted by such court .
Any order of the Franklin Circuit Court shall be
enforceable and shall be valid anywhere in this state
and the order of either court shall be reviewable as
provided in the Rules of Civil Procedure in the case of
other injunctions and restraining orders . (1960 , c.
218 , § 21 ; effective June 16, 1960 )
323.990 [ 73-14]
PENALTIES .
Any person who violates any provision of this chapter
shall be fined not less than one hundred dollars nor
more than five hundred dollars , or imprisoned not
more than three months , or both . (1960 , с . 218, §
22; effective June 16, 1960 )
17

FOR INFORMATION ONLY
REGULATIONS
OF THE
STATE BOARD OF EXAMINERS
AND REGISTRATION OF ARCHITECTS
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
FOR INFORMATION ONLY
19

PURSUANT TO THE AUTHORITY VESTED
IN THE STATE BOARD OF EXAMINERS
AND REGISTRATION OF ARCHITECTS BY
KRS 323.210 . THE FOLLOWING REGU-
LATIONS WERE ADOPTED MAY 20, 1960 .
20

BERA 15
RE : APPLICATIONS FOR REGISTRATION
Relates to KRS 323.050 Applications for registration
will be received at all times , but applications for
examinations must be in the hands of the Secretary
not less than thirty (30) days prior to the date of the
examination . No application will be accepted or
considered unless made on the printed forms issued
by the board and in strict accordance with the
"Instructions to Applicants " contained thereon .
BERA 16
RE : APPROVED SCHOOLS AND COLLEGES
Relates to KRS 323.050 Schools and Colleges meet-
ing the requirements of the National Architectural
Accrediting Board shall constitute the list of those
approved by this Board .
BERA 17
RE : EXAMINATIONS
Relates to KRS 323.050 DEFINITION OF AССЕРТА-
BLE ARCHITECTURAL EXPERIENCE REQUIRED OF ALL
APPLICANTS FOR EXAMINATION . Any person to be
eligible for examination under the Kentucky Revised
Statutes covering the operation of this board must
present authentic evidence , satisfactory to the board ,
showing that he has had responsible charge , under
the supervision of a registered architect , of all
branches of architectural practice , including design ,
preparation of working drawings , specifications , con-
ferences with the Owner , taking of bids , and super-
vision and all administration through the entirety of
the project or projects .
Section 2. WHEN TO SUBMIT APPLICATIONS : All
applications for examination must be in the hands of
the Secretary not less than thirty (30) days prior to
the date of such examination .
21

Section 3. APPLICATION BY THOSE WHO HAVE
BEEN PREVIOUSLY DECLINED OR BY CANDIDATES
WHO DO NOT ATTEND THE FIRST EXAMINATIONS
TO WHICH THEY ARE ADMITTED OR THOSE SEEK-
ING REGISTRATION BY RECIPROCITY : Application
for examination by architects already registered in
Kentucky , or by candidates who do not attend the
first examination to which they are admitted or by
opplicants whose original applications were declined
or by applicants who failed to pass their first ex-
amination or by those seeking registration by reci-
procity shall be made by letter to the Secretary of the
Board and no additional fee shall be charged for
such application or examination . Provided , however ,
that such request be made within the same time
limits as above set forth and within a period of three
years from date of the original application . Other-
wise application must be made in the manner of
original applications and be accompanied by the
usual fee .
Section 4. GENERAL PROVISIONS . All examinations
shall be held in the English language , without the
use of an interpreter and under such auspices and
environment as will insure their proper conduct
under dignified surroundings .
Section 5. TYPES OF EXAMINATION GIVEN : The
board gives two types of examinations , namely the
written examination and the Senior examination .
Those eligible for the two types of examinations are
as follows : See Section 9 ( BERA 17) and Section 13
(BERA 17)
SECTION 6. TIME AND NOTICE OF HOLDING
WRITTEN EXAMINATIONS : LENGTH OF SUCH
EXAMINATIONS : Written examinations will be held
at a time and place designated by the board when-
ever a sufficient number of candidates requesting
the examination warrants holding such an examina-
tion . However , in no case shall a candidate be
denied the privilege of being given an examination
for a period greater than six (6) months after date
22

of application in the event that there are no other
applicants . Candidates will be notified of time and
place of examination at least fourteen ( 14) days
prior to holding of the examination . Written ex-
aminations will be conducted over a period of four
consecutive days , a total of 36 hours , three days of
eight hours each and one day of twelve hours in
accordance with the Syllabus of the Standard NCARB
examination as outlined in the Circulars of Advice
of the National Council of Architectural Registration
Boards .
Section 7. REPORT FOR WRITTEN EXAMINATION :
All candidates shall present themselves promptly at
8:00 o'clock on the morning of the first day of the
examinations for instructions , at a place designated
by the board .
Section 8. MATERIAL REQUIRED FOR WRITTEN EX-
AMINATIONS : The applicant shall bring all neces-
sary drawing instruments , T-squares , triangles , scales ,
erasers , pencils and reference books . Drawing boards
and all necessary paper and scratch pads will be
supplied by the board .
Section 9. ELIGIBILITY TO TAKE WRITTEN EXAM-
INATIONS : Any person who possesses the qualifica-
tions prescribed by KRS 323.050 and who has never
been registered as an architect in Kentucky , and
any person so qualified who has been registered in
Kentucky without examination , but wishes to change
his status to registration by examination shall be
eligible to take the Written Examination .
Eligibility to acquire the National Council of Archi-
tectural Registration Boards Certificate through re-
ceipt of credits from the state examinations must be
approved by the Council prior to formal application
to the Kentucky Board through the Council on the
basis of a completed Council Record .
Section 10. GENERAL SCOPE OF THE WRITTEN
EXAMINATION : The Written examination shall
cover , in addition to the grading of the Candidate's
23

Council Record , written and graphic examinations ex-
tending over a period of thirty -six (36) hours , divided
into three days of eight (8) hours each and one day
of twelve ( 12) hours . The total value of the examina-
tion shall be 1000 points , and is made up of the
following divisions .
Section 11. THE SUBJECTS COVERED BY THE
WRITTEN EXAMINATION : (Examinations A and B.
to be taken only by candidates for the Standard
NCARB Examinations .)
EXAMINATION A- ACADEMIC AND PRACTICAL
TRAINING .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
Records of education , employment , experience and
ethical standing . The examining body shall scrutinize
and grade the candidate's record in preparatory
school , cultural and technical college , employment
experience , and professional society affiliations , as
collected , collated and certified by the National
Council of Architectural Registration Boards .
EXAMINATION B- PERSONAL AUDIENCE .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
The candidates shall appear personally before the
examining body so that it may have an opportunity
to judge his natural endowments for the practice of
architecture , his ethical standards , and by questions ,
gain further knowledge of his fitness . The time for
this audience may be set by the examining body .
EXAMINATION C-HISTORY AND THEORY OF
ARCHITECTURE .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
Time : 3 hours .
No reference material permitted .
Subject matter : The development of architecture ; the
effect of geography , climate , and material resources ;
the factors of site and orientation ; the influence of
24

science and technology . The varying response to the
needs and desires of the changing social pattern ; the
evolution of design , methods of construction , struc-
tural systems and building equipment ; the influence
of tradition and law . Typical historical examples .
EXAMINATION D-SITE PLANNING .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
Time : 5 hours .
No reference material permitted .
Subject matter : A short design problem involving a
group of public or private buildings of any type ,
intended to demonstrate the candidate's ability to
safeguard the interest of both client and public with
respect to land value, obsolescence , traffic, to de-
velop a functional arrangement of buildings and site
which will also result in a visually satisfying composi-
tion of forms in space .
EXAMINATION E- ARCHITECTURAL DESIGN .
Value 200 points , passing 150 points .
Time : 12 hours .
No reference material permitted .
Subject matter : A specific practical problem involving
application of the principles of space design to solu-
tion of the program for a building such as might be
found in architectural practice . The solution shall be
submitted in drawings of specified number , kind and
scale to present an efficient arrangement , logical
structural organizations , compliance with basic codes
for health and safety ; also appropriate economy and
satisfying aesthetic quality .
EXAMINATION F- BUILDING CONSTRUCTION .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
Time : 3 hours .
No reference material permitted .
Subject matter : The use of materials and methods of
construction . The selection of appropriate materials
and knowledge of how they are combined for use
25

under given conditions , considering their practicality
and durability , also the factors of safety , economy ,
and architectural expression . Supervision of construc-
tion ; detection and correction of defects ; sequence of
construction operations .
EXAMINATION G-STRUCTURAL DESIGN .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
Time : 5 hours .
Reference material is permitted .
Subject matter : The knowledge of various structural
systems when , how, and why each would be used ;
the determination of safe and efficient sizes of struc-
tural elements and arrangements of combinations of
such elements as slabs , beams , girders , trusses , col-
umns, walls , foundations , and footings , designed in
various materials by either graphical or mathematical
analysis.
EXAMINATION H- PROFESSIONAL ADMINISTRA-
TION .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
Time : 3 hours .
No reference material permitted .
Subject matter : Questions relating to the ethical ,
legal and administrative responsibilities of the archi-
tect; relationships between architect , owner, and con-
tractor ; building laws , lien laws , specifications , con-
tracts , bonds , insurance , certificates and arbitration .
EXAMINATION I- BUILDING EQUIPMENT .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
Time : 5 hours .
Use of handbooks permitted .
Subject matter : Principles of design and detailed
knowledge concerning heating , ventilating , air con-
ditioning, plumbing , electric wiring , refrigeration , fire
protection , and vertical transportation ; taking into
account scientific , technical , economic , and practical
factors ; also compliance with codes for public health
and safety.
26

Section 12. RETAKING WRITTEN EXAMINATION . If
the point value of the parts of the Written examina-
tion passed exceed the point value of those failed ,
the candidate need elect to retake only the subjects
in which he failed , if done within one year from
date . Otherwise , the candidate must retake all sub-
jects if he elects to try again .
Section 13. DEFINITION OF SENIOR EXAMINA-
TIONS : TO WHOM GIVEN : Senior examinations
(oral ) given to registered architects of Kentucky or
registered architects of other states having reciprocal
licensing laws will be given during any regular or
special meeting of the board . Such examinations con-
sist of submission of required exhibits with application
plus a hearing before the board . Rating will be in
accordance with the Syllabus of the Standard NCARB
examinations as outlined in the Circulars of Advice of
the National Council of Architectural Registration
Boards .
Section 14. ELIGIBILITY TO TAKE SENIOR EXAMINA-
TIONS: Any person who is already registered as
an architect in Kentucky or in any other state having
reciprocal licensing laws and who wishes to establish
a Certificate of Registration with the National Coun-
cilof Architectural Registration Boards shall be eligi-
ble
totake the Senior Examination
, provided he
shows by his application
tothe satisfaction
ofthe
board
:
a. that he has an educational background which
is
satisfactory
tothe board
.
b. that he has had five
(5) years
ormore proven
practice
as
aregistered architect and as
aprincipal
orone
of
agroup
ofprincipals
incharge of an archi-
tect's office engaged
inthe practice
ofarchitecture
as
aprofession
, by performing
orsupervising the
performance
ofthe five fundamental functions
ofan
architect
, namely
:
1.The study and delineation
ofbuilding problems
commonly called
"preliminary studies
."
27

2. The preparation of working drawings .
3. Specifications .
4. Detail drawings .
5. General supervision of construction , including the
taking of bids, preparation of contracts , auditing of
accounts and the arbitration of differences between
contractors and between contractors and owners .
c. That his practice has been honorable and discreet
as evidenced by the testimony of not less than three
clients , not less than three fellow - architects , public
records and authentic photographs of executed work .
In determining what constitutes the practice of archi-
ture as a principal or as one of a group of principals ,
the remuneration which the applicant may have re-
ceived for his services , whether by way of commis-
sion , fixed fee or salary , shall not be determinative .
On the contrary , the board shall determine from the
evidence submitted whether in
itsjudgment the ap-
plicant has been
acontrolling officer
incharge
of
the work purported
tohave been executed by him
,
whether he has had the power
toemploy and
discharge draftsmen
, superintendents
, specification
writers
, and other employees
ofthe office
oroffices
inwhich he has practiced
, and whether
ornot the
work the applicant executed has been put out under
his name and seal as
"Architect
"
insuch
amanner
that the applicant assumed legal responsibility for
its
accuracy and quality
.
Section 15. SUBJECTS COVERED BY THE SENIOR
EXAMINATIONS
.
EXAMINATION
J-GENERAL CULTURAL EDUCATION
.
Value 100 points
, passing 75 points
.
An evaluation
ofthe applicant's education
inhigh
school
. Liberal Arts courses
, Travel
, Self
-study
inthe
Humanities
, and participation
incultural activities
.
28

EXAMINATION K-TECHNICAL TRAINING
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
An evaluation of the applicant's training in Architec-
tural School , Office , and in independent research and
investigation .
EXAMINATION L- RECORD IN PRACTICE AND PRO-
FESSIONAL RELATIONSHIPS .
Value 100 points , passing 75 points .
An evaluation of the applicant's record of activity in
professional societies , relations with clients , ethical
standards , civic activity and journalistic contributions .
EXAMINATION M- EXHIBITS AND ORAL DISCUS-
SION .
Value 300 points , passing 225 points .
Explanation by candidate of his approach to archi-
tecture , his personal contribution to the work shown
in his exhibits , and his relations with clients , contrac-
tors and public .
EXAMINATION N- ORAL THESIS .
Value 400 points , passing 300 points .
Time : At least 30 minutes .
A demonstration of the candidate's philosophy and
procedure, based on some project or projects which
he has executed , or which are in an advanced state
of study , illustrated by sketches , working drawings,
and photographs.
(Grades in Examinations J , K, and L are based upon
information contained in the candidate's Council
Records.)
For Examinations M and N the candidate is required
to appear before the examining body with complete
sets of plans and specifications for at least three dif-
ferent examples of his executed work plus any
sketches or photographs desired .
29

Section 16. WHEN APPLICANTS MAY BE EXCUSED
FROM PART OF SENIOR EXAMINATION : The board
may , in
itsdiscretion
, excuse any eligible applicant
for the Senior Examination from any part
orparts
inwhich
it
issufficiently shown
, by his application
and record
, that he
isqualified
.
Section 17. TAKING SENIOR EXAMINATION TO
SECURE NCARB REGISTRATION
:
Ifany applicant
shall desire to take the Senior Examination for the
purpose
ofacquiring
aCertificate
ofRegistration from
the National Council
ofArchitectural Registration
Boards rather than from state only
, he shall first
establish his eligibility
onthe basis
ofan approved
Council Record and submit through the Council an
official NCARB application
tothe Kentucky Board
.
Section 18. SUBSTITUTING WRITTEN FOR SENIOR
EXAMINATIONS
: Candidates eligible for the Senior
examination may
,
ifthey
sodesire
, substitute the
Written examination
.
Section 19. REPEATING SENIOR EXAMINATIONS
:
Any applicant who has taken the Senior Examination
and failed
topass
,who
issubsequently admitted
to
another Senior examination
, may be required
to
repeat the entire examination
oronly such
ofthe
parts
asmay be designated by the Board
.
Section 20. POSTPONEMENTS AND SPECIAL EX-
AMINATIONS
: The Board may postpone any exami-
nation
orprovide for special meetings and exami-
nations where the number
ofapplicants warrant
it;
provided
, however
, that
atleast one examination
during the course
ofthe year shall be held
inboth
the Written and Senior classes
ifone
ormore appli-
cations are approved
.
Section 21. KENTUCKY ARCHITECTS APPLYING FOR
THE STANDARD NCARB EXAMINATIONS
: Candi-
dates who are residents of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and who wish
toqualify for the Standard
NCARB examination must make application through
30

the Council as required of non - resident applicants .
Provisions relating to the payment of fees required
in Kentucky are the same as outlined in Section 18
of the Regulation BERA 7, but do not effect the pay-
ment of Council fees .
Section 22. FAIRNESS IN GRADING : In order to pre-
serve the anonymity of the candidate until the ex-
amining committee has rated the papers and exhibits ,
each candidate shall be assigned a number with
which he shall mark all papers and exhibits .
Section 23. FEES FURTHER DEFINED : Under KRS
323.080 , two separate and distinct fees of thirty - five
($35.00) dollars and twenty - five ($ 25.00 ) dollars ,
respectively , are called for- and examination fee of
thirty- five ($ 35.00 ) dollars and a registration fee of
twenty - five ( $ 25.00 ) dollars.
BERA 18
RE : REGISTRATION OF NON -RESIDENT
ARCHITECTS
Relates to KRS 323.060 GENERAL INFORMATION :
Non - resident architects must be fully registered in
Kentucky before they come into this state to seek
work or to represent themselves as architects . As some
time may be required in securing such registration ,
application should be made well in advance of any
contemplated practice. It is the express intention of
the Kentucky Board to register all qualified appli-
cants, but it can not act without a complete record
at hand . To grant even temporary registration , with-
out such a record , would tend to defeat unprejudiced
consideration of the final application . In the event
of rejection untold and unnecessary hardship would
result to all parties involved and serious complications
arise . The board , therefore looks with disfavor upon
any lack of cooperation in this respect , and counts
it as a mark of discredit upon any applicant . The
board will register all architects who fully comply
with
itsrules and regulations
, but temporary regis
31

tration or permits are not granted . Architects must
be registered before coming into Kentucky to get
work . Their applications must be filed with the re-
quired fees well in advance of the contemplated
work.
The board has ruled that there is no excuse for an
architect to seek work in Kentucky and then apply
for registration after receiving the work .
Any person not a resident of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky or who has not been a resident of this
Commonwealth for at least one year immediately
preceding application , who desires registration in
this state , is required to make application through
the National Council of Architectural Registration
Boards , 521 Eighteenth St. N.W., Washington , D.C.
20006 , of which this board is a member , except
persons who are residents of and licensed by a
state in which , by law , it is impossible to make
application through said National Council by way
of the licensing authority of that state . The appli-
cation of each person , who , for the reason just
stated, can not make application through said Na-
tional Council , shall be individually considered ,
and the applicant will be registered if, at the time
he was licensed in the state of his residency , the
qualifications prescribed for licensing by that
state were , in the opinion of the board , equal to
those prescribed in this Commonwealth at the date
of such application , and if reciprocal licensing
privileges satisfactory to the board are granted by
such other state to licensees of this Common-
wealth . Contrary to popular belief, making applica-
tion through the NCARB does not involve any
delay or detail which would not be encountered if
application were made direct to the Kentucky Board .
The Council simply attends to having the necessary
application forms properly completed , collects the
fees , and makes the same investigation of the appli-
cant's record which would otherwise have to be made
by the Kentucky Board and for which the NCARB
32

is a great deal better equipped . The Council office
does not give examinations .
Once a " Council Record " has been prepared for an
applicant , the original is kept at the office and trans-
fer may be made to other states by means of copies
of this same record. This simplification of process in
securing additional transfers would not be possible
except through the Council. Applicants who must take
their first examination , or an additional examination
to comply with the requirements of another state ,
or who wish to complete the Standard Council Ex-
amination , as hereafter described , should take such
examinations or additional subjects in their own state ,
thus saving the time and expense of a long trip .
The only basis of transfer of registration to Kentucky
from another state are examination credits and a
consideration of the moral and ethical status of ap-
plicants , all of which are to be determined upon the
merits of each individual case by the Kentucky Board .
If the applicant has never passed an examination ,
then he should apply for and enter the Senior or
Written examinations in his own state either by direct
application to his state board or by application to
his state board through NCARB . Since the Council
Record (and Certificate ) are required before making
application for reciprocal registration to the Kentucky
Board , duplication in establishing state eligibility and
NCARB eligibility may be eliminated by making the
application through the Council.
The Board , may, in
itsdiscretion
:
(a)give such credit
for examinations already successfully passed as
it
deems fair and just and
inaccordance with
itsstand-
ards
, and after giving due weight
toexperience
subsequent
toexamination
, require an applicant
to
pass whatever additional examinations are necessary
,
inthe judgment
ofthis board
,
tobring the applicant's
qualifications up
tothe standard required
inKen-
tucky
;
(b)call for personal questioning any applicant
whose record
ofmoral
, ethical
, technical or other
professional qualification
isnot sufficiently clear
or
33

satisfactory ; (c) grant registration without examina-
tion to any architect whose application and Council
Certificate show him to be qualified and so fitted to
practice architecture that his services will , in the judg-
ment of the board , be a valuable asset to both the
Commonwealth of Kentucky and the profession of
architecture in this state .
Section 2. THE STANDARD NCARB EXAMINATION :
The examinations given by the board are in compli-
ance with the Standard NCARB examinations . Al-
though the policies of the Kentucky Board allow
reciprocity with most other states , the board recom-
mends that anyone applying for examination in Ken-
tucky consider the advantages of applying for the
standard NCARB examination . If eligibility is estab-
lished with NCARB , the successful completion of the
Standard NCARB examinations are the basis for issu-
ance of the NCARB Certificate usually necessary
for registration in other states . Information concerning
method of application for such an examination may
be obtained by writing the office of the Board .
BERA 19
RE : ANNUAL RENEWAL FEE
Relates to KRS 323.110 The renewal fee shall be
due and paid on or before the first day of July each
year . Anyone failing to pay the annual fee within
sixty (60) days after receiving notice of fees due
shall be assessed a penalty of five ($5.00 ) dollars .
Anyone failing to pay the annual fee plus the penalty
on or before October 1st is guilty of violation of
the law and is subject to action by the board to
revoke his certificate ; or , the board may in
itsdis-
cretion declare the registration
ofany such person
tobe voluntarily surrendered under the provisions
of KRS 323.110
. Certificates granted between July
1
and January
1 shall be first renewed on
orbefore
the first day
ofthe July following
. Certificates
granted on
orbetween the first day
ofJanuary
of
34

any year and the first day of July shall be first
renewed on or before the first day of July in the year
following the granting of such certificates .
Section 2. During a period of active military duty,
an architect in the service , upon duly notifying the
board , may be excused from paying the annual re-
newal fee until such time as his military service is
terminated and he wishes to resume practice . An
identification card will be issued upon notification of
the return from duty upon payment of the current
annual renewal fee.
BERA 20
RE : DUPLICATE CERTIFICATES
Relates to KRS 323.020 and KRS 323.110 Holders
of Certificates of registration in Kentucky may secure
duplicate certificates upon payment of a fee of
ten ($ 10.00 ) dollars to cover the expense of issuing .
Provided, however , that no such duplicate certificates
shall be issued except by order of the board .
BERA 21
RE : INDIVIDUAL SEALS
Relates to KRS 323.095 Each architect registered
for practice within the Commonwealth of Kentucky
shall sign all documents prepared by him or under
his supervision and shall secure and use an embossed
seal of the following approved design :
To consist of two concentric circles , the outer circle
to be one and nine -sixteenth (1-9/ 16 ") inches in di-
ameter and the inner circle to be one (1 ") inch in
diameter . The upper portion of the annular space
between the two circles shall bear the name of the
registrant . The lower portion of the annular space
shall bear the word "Architect " . The space enclosed
by the inner circle shall be divided into an upper
and lower half . The upper portion shall consist of
35

three lines , the word " Registered ", then the abbrevia-
tion " No. " , and then the number of the Certificate
of the Registrant . The lower portion shall contain , in
three lines , the words " Commonwealth of Kentucky ."
BERA 22
RE : STANDARDS OF PLANS AND
SPECIFICATIONS
Relates to KRS 323.010 (3 ) All plans and specifica-
tions prepared by an architect for use within the
Commonwealth of Kentucky shall be of a standard
sufficient to safeguard the public against misinterpre-
tations and shall show the essential parts of the
design and details necessary to assure safety in
construction .
BERA 23
RE : BY -LAWS
Relates to KRS 323.180 and 323.210 MEETINGS :
Regular meetings of the State Board of Examiners
and Registration of Architects shall be held in the
months of January , July and October of each year
and the July meeting shall be known as the Annual
Meeting . Special meetings may be called by the
President and the Secretary or by filing with the
Secretary the written request of any three members
of the Board .
Meetings shall be held at such time and place as
the board may designate . Where the board has not
fixed the time or place for holding any meeting , the
President shall have the power to designate both
the time and place .
Section 2- ELECTION OF OFFICERS : The election of
officers shall take place at the Annual Meeting in
July and the Board shall elect from among
itsmem-
bers
,
apresident
,
asecretary and
atreasurer
. The
offices
ofsecretary and treasurer may be held by
the same person
.
36

Section 3-OFFICE OF THE BOARD : The office of
the Board shall be at the office of the Secretary , or
at such other place as may be designated by the
action of the Board .
Section 4- SALARIES : Members of the Board shall
receive a salary of twenty - five ($25.00 ) dollars per
day for
alltime actually expended
inthe pursuance
ofthe duties imposed upon them
, including time
spent
innecessary travel
, and
inaddition all neces-
sary traveling and incidental expenses incurred
in
connection with said duties
. To cover general routine
work
ofthe office
, the Secretary shall receive
,
in
addition
tothe per diem and traveling expenses
, the
sum
ofone hundred
($100.00
) dollars per month
.

5
-THE PRESIDENT

, when

atall meetings
,appoint all commit-

toconfirmation by vote
ofthe board
,
sign all certificates issued and perform all other duties
ordinarily pertaining

,

Section
6
-THE SECRETARY
: The Secretary shall

Section
7
-THE TREASURER
: The Treasurer shall re-
ceive and deposit all fees
,make disbursements subject
tothe board's approval
oras the law directs and
keep all accounts and records relating
tofinance
37

Such letter ballots shall provide for a negative vote ,
an affirmative vote and a vote to defer action on
the question until the next board meeting .
One member , or more voting to defer action , shall
obligate the Secretary to throw out the vote and
present a second letter ballot to determine whether
a special meeting shall be called for consideration
of this question or the matter deferred until the next
scheduled meeting . If a special meeting is voted , then
the President shall designate the date, time and place
for holding such meeting .
Individual letter ballots shall be tabulated by the
Secretary ten days after date of mailing and ballots
received after that time shall not be counted .
Single letter ballots , providing for vote of all mem-
bers of the board on the same sheet , may be issued
whenever it is necessary that important papers or
other exhibits accompany the ballot for inspection
or consideration before voting . Single letter ballots
shall be tabulated upon their return to the office
of the board .
Section 11 - REPORTS : All written committee or other
official reports shall be upon the forms of the board
and placed for record in the proper order of their
submission .
Section 12- CERTIFICATES AND CARDS : Certificates
of Registration of such form and size as the Board
may approve and adopt shall be given to each
registrant to be held until such time as the certificate
may expire or be revoked by the board . In addition
the board shall issue to each holder of a Certificate
of Registration , upon payment of the proper fees ,
a card to contain the name , certificate number and
date of expiration of such certificate .
Section 13-BOOKS AND RECORDS: The following
shall be kept by the Secretary and Treasurer and
constitute the official records and books of the Board:
39

(1) MINUTES AND REPORTS -a looseleaf book to
contain , in proper order, all minutes and official
reports.
(2) CHRONOLOGICAL RECORD - a looseleaf book
to contain brief summaries of the date of receipt and
disposition of applicants, also yearly summaries in a
clear and concise manner .
(3) ALPHABETICAL RECORD - a looseleaf book to
contain individual outline records of each applicant,
in alphabetical order, beginning with the receipt of
the application and continuing perpetually from that
date . Each record shall include notations concerning
payment of all fees received .
(4) FILING SYSTEM-arranged so as to properly
care for the filing of applicant's papers, bills and
receipts , general correspondence , material concern-
ing the laws and procedure of other states and all
other papers which are to be temporarily and/or
permanently perserved .
(5) CASH BOOK -to show receipts and disburse-
ments .
Section 14-AMENDMENTS : The board's rules and
regulations including these By-Laws may be amended
at any regular or special meeting of the Board by
regular vote provided a copy of the proposed amend-
ments have been given or mailed to each member
of the Board at least ten days prior to the date of
the meeting in which they are to be considered , or
without such notice at any meeting where there is a
full attendance of all five members of the Board .
Amendments by letter ballot may be passed , pro-
vided all five members vote upon the questions in-
volved . Less than five votes shall be construed in the
same manner as though those not voting had voted
to defer action until the next meeting .
40

BERA 24
RE : REGULATIONS
Relates to KRS 323.210 Regulation BERA I through
BERA XIII is hereby repealed .
41
